ELPAC Level 4
Lexile 1000–1300L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Hook, No Portrait Yet

STS-MS8-L4-10001300-MSSS8-MEC-IFN-01 • MS Social Studies Grade 8 • The Civil War
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
A museum curator decides what goes in the case and what the little card next to it says. That is real power, because the label is the story most visitors will remember. When this gallery's student curators researched 1865, they found a name that had been left off the old exhibit: Ely S. Parker, a Seneca engineer and Union officer, wrote out the final surrender terms at Appomattox in his own handwriting. Native nations fought, negotiated, and lost land on every side of this war, and most exhibits never say so.
If you were curating this case, which object would you put in the very front, and what would your label say about it?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Wenona doing?
Look at the picture. Say what Wenona is doing and justify it with evidence you can see. Speak in three or more complete sentences.
2
What is Tahoma holding?
Look at the picture. Say what Tahoma has in his hands and justify it with evidence you can see. Speak in three or more complete sentences.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say what the teacher is doing and justify it with evidence you can see. Speak in three or more complete sentences.
4
Where are these people? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say where these people are and justify it with two pieces of evidence you can see. Speak in three or more complete sentences.
5
Tell me about the exhibit wall.
Look at the picture. Say what the long white wall shows and justify what it teaches a visitor. Speak in three or more complete sentences.
6
Tell me about what Aiyana is wearing.
Look at the picture. Say what Aiyana has on and justify what her task in this room is. Speak in three or more complete sentences.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: HSS 8.10 — The Civil War. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
